stem: A water pump fills a storage tank at a rate of 45 gallons per minute. The tank has a capacity of 2,000 liters. Approximately how many minutes will it take the pump to fill the tank completely? (Use 1 gallon = 3.785 liters)
explanation:
The tank capacity is in liters but the pump rate is in gallons per minute. The student must convert the tank capacity to gallons before dividing by the pump rate.

Step 1 — Convert tank capacity from liters to gallons:
$$2{,}000 \text{ L} \times \frac{1 \text{ gal}}{3.785 \text{ L}} = \frac{2{,}000}{3.785} \approx 528.4 \text{ gal}$$

Step 2 — Divide by the pump rate to find time:
$$\frac{528.4 \text{ gal}}{45 \text{ gal/min}} \approx 11.74 \text{ min}$$

Full chain with unit cancellation:
$$2{,}000 \text{ L} \times \frac{1 \text{ gal}}{3.785 \text{ L}} \times \frac{1 \text{ min}}{45 \text{ gal}} = \frac{2{,}000}{3.785 \times 45} = \frac{2{,}000}{170.325} \approx 11.7 \text{ min}$$

The pump will fill the tank in approximately $\boxed{11.7}$ minutes.

distractorRationale.A: Wrong conversion factor error: uses 1 gallon = 4 liters (a plausible rounded value) instead of 3.785 liters. Calculation: $2{,}000 \div 4 \div 45 = 500 \div 45 \approx 11.1$ min. A student who approximates 1 gallon as exactly 4 liters produces this answer.
distractorRationale.B: Correct answer. Divides 2,000 liters by 3.785 L/gal to get gallons, then divides by 45 gal/min.
distractorRationale.C: Forgotten unit conversion error (stops one step early): the student divides the tank capacity in liters directly by the pump rate in gallons per minute, without converting liters to gallons. Calculation: $2{,}000 \div 45 \approx 44.4$ min. The unit mismatch (liters vs. gallons) is never addressed.
distractorRationale.D: Inverted conversion error: the student multiplies by 3.785 instead of dividing, converting liters to a larger number of units rather than a smaller number of gallons. Calculation: $2{,}000 \times 3.785 \div 45 = 7{,}570 \div 45 \approx 168.2$ min.
